steps.name: Integrate rust_lisp into alfred-lisp
criteria: rust_lisp added as dependency to alfred-lisp
A runtime can be instantiated and is ready to evaluate Lisp expressions
eval function parses and evaluates a Lisp string
Arithmetic expressions evaluate correctly
Syntax errors return Result Err not panic

steps.name: Bridge registers buffer primitives
criteria: buffer-insert primitive adds text at cursor position
buffer-delete primitive removes character at cursor
buffer-content primitive returns buffer text as string
cursor-position primitive returns line and column
Primitives registered at runtime startup

steps.name: Bridge registers cursor and message primitives
criteria: cursor-move primitive moves cursor by direction and count
message primitive sets the editor message line
current-mode primitive returns the current mode name
All primitives accessible from Lisp evaluation
Wrong argument types return error messages

steps.name: Wire Lisp runtime into event loop
criteria: LispRuntime created and initialized in main
Colon command mode can evaluate Lisp via :eval prefix
Lisp errors display as messages not crashes
Editor state accessible to Lisp primitives during eval
eval-file function loads and evaluates a Lisp file

steps.name: Performance baseline and kill signal check
criteria: Benchmark test measures single primitive eval latency
Latency under 1ms for buffer-insert call
Latency under 1ms for cursor-move call
Results documented in test output
Kill signal: if over 1ms evaluate Janet alternative
